title: Pre-execution monitors — gate agent completion on required tool calls before final output
body: gsd-2's discuss.md prompt has a 'mandatory investigation pass' — the agent MUST scout the codebase, check library docs, and web search before asking its first question. This is articulated in the prompt but not enforced by the framework. In our framework, a pre-execution monitor could gate agent completion by checking whether required tool calls have been made (e.g., 'this agent must have called grep or read at least N files before submitting final output'). The prompt encodes the intent; the monitor enforces it. This is a specific application of the monitor classify pattern: the collector observes tool_results during the agent's execution, the classifier checks whether required investigation tools were called, FLAG verdict prevents premature completion. Generalizes to any agent quality gate based on observable execution behavior before output submission.
